close_reason: Complete visual overhaul of The Two of Us V2. All 10 scenes converted to flow image layout with Gemini-generated art. New Explainer panel added. Narrative reordered. Copy workshopped. RainbowText shadow fix discovered and stored.
description:
Continuing scene-by-scene visual polish of The Two of Us V2 manhwa scroll story at docs/the-two-of-us-v2/. 

STATUS: Scenes 01 and 02 are DONE. Resume at Scene 03.

USER'S CORE RULES (non-negotiable):
1. NO opacity/fade animations on ANY text — text must always be 100% opaque
2. NO transitionary fading — no framer-motion opacity on text elements
3. RainbowText (background-clip:text gradient) appears BLACK on dark backgrounds — needs strong drop-shadow or scrim behind it
4. Position text in the dark/negative space WITHIN the AI art panel, not floating outside it
5. All text must pop like manhwa text — bold, crisp, fully readable

WHAT WAS DONE (sessions 1-2):
- Stripped all SVG/CSS illustrations from all 10 scenes (AI Art Hero pass)
- Removed scroll-behavior:smooth, overflow-x:hidden, overscroll-behavior:none from html/body
- Removed overflow:hidden from .scene sections
- Removed ALL framer-motion parallax (useScroll/useTransform) from Scene.tsx
- Removed ALL opacity animations from text elements across all scenes
- Added multi-layer text-shadow to all text, bumped font-weights, brightened colors
- RainbowText: triple drop-shadow filter (text-shadow doesn't work with background-clip:text)
- Scene 02: repositioned text into dark pocket (left-aligned, inside panel, margin-left: 30px, justify-content: flex-end, padding-bottom: 180px)
- Scene 03: chat bubble backgrounds boosted to 0.35/0.30 alpha
- Scene 05: dark scrim + backdrop-blur behind step labels and caption
- Scene 06: dark scrim + backdrop-blur behind caption
- Scene 08: AI image opacity reduced to 0.28, radial gradient scrim behind mainText, text colors brightened
- Scene 09: teamOld opacity 0.55, comparison box border 0.15, arrow brightened

APPROACH FOR REMAINING SCENES:
- Screenshot each scene via Playwright MCP (localhost:5174)
- Show user, get feedback, iterate positioning/readability
- Text must sit in negative space within the AI illustration
- Use dark scrims (rgba bg + backdrop-blur) where needed for readability
- No opacity animations on text ever

KEY FILES: docs/the-two-of-us-v2/src/scenes/Scene{03-10}*.tsx and *.module.css
TOOLS: Playwright MCP for screenshots (browser_navigate, browser_evaluate for scrolling, browser_take_screenshot)
notes:
Session 3 progress (2026-03-04):

SCENES COMPLETED:
- Scene 01: Regenerated art via Nano Banana Pro (Gemini). Removed all SVG hat icons, floating animations, and ParticleField — everything baked into AI art. New layout: flow image (artContainer + max-width:700px), no absolute positioning.
- Scene 03: Regenerated art via Nano Banana Pro. Male protagonist (was female). Speech bubbles baked into art. Removed HTML overlay bubbles. Same flow image layout.
- Scene 04: Full opacity art, flow image layout (was cropped by object-fit:cover). Caption in absolute captionWrap. Text overlay with RainbowText title.

KEY PATTERN ESTABLISHED:
All scenes now use same layout: sceneInner > artContainer (relative, max-width:700px) > img (display:block, width:100%, height:auto) + overlay div (absolute inset:0). This shows full portrait art at natural aspect ratio. Caption via captionWrap (absolute bottom:5%).

ART GENERATION:
- Using Nano Banana Pro (gemini-3-pro-image-preview) via Gemini API for best quality
- User generates manually in Gemini chat, downloads PNG, we swap in
- Prompts should include all text/labels/captions to bake into art
- 9:16 aspect ratio, manhwa style, consistent male protagonist (dark messy hair, dark jacket/hoodie)

REMAINING: Scenes 02, 05-10 still need the same treatment (full art layout + potential art regen)

FILES CHANGED:
- Scene01Hats.tsx + .module.css (simplified)
- Scene03Conversation.tsx + .module.css (simplified)
- Scene04Office.tsx + .module.css (flow image layout)
- public/scenes/scene-01-hats.png (new Gemini art)
- public/scenes/scene-03-conversation.png (new Gemini art)
title: Polish: scene-by-scene visual audit (panels 3-10)

description: Inspired by Wispr Flow deep scan (docs/competitors/wisprflow/deep-scan-2026-03.md §6). They have 90+ notification types — we don't need that many, but we should cover the actionable error states: mic disconnected mid-recording, no audio detected during dictation, paste failed/blocked by target app, Bluetooth audio quality warnings, wrong language setting detected, accessibility permission revoked. Each notification should give the user a specific fix action, not a generic error. See also feature-requests/021-bluetooth-audio-resilience.md for Bluetooth-specific handling.
title: Contextual error notifications for common failure modes

description: Let users choose between speed and quality. small.en (244MB, fast), medium.en (769MB, balanced), large-v3 (2.9GB, best). Each has different download size and RTF. Update WhisperKitSetupService to handle multiple variants. Update SpeechEngineSettingsView with model picker (only when WhisperKit selected). Ref: whisperkit-research.md Model Variants section.
notes: Decided: large-v3 only. No cheaper model options. Users get the best quality, period.
title: WhisperKit: Offer model size choices (small.en / medium.en / large-v3)

description:
Finding Q8: brain-check.sh only scans .claude/ directory for broken markdown links. CLAUDE.md at project root contains many links to .claude/knowledge/ and .claude/skills/ files that go unchecked.

DEFERRED: CLAUDE.md links are relatively stable and manually maintained. Adding root scanning changes the script's scope.
UPSIDE: Catches broken links in the most important brain file.
CHURN RISK: Low — just extend the find path to include CLAUDE.md. But CLAUDE.md has many links to skills/agents which are gitignored and may not exist in fresh clones.
REVISIT TRIGGER: If a broken CLAUDE.md link causes incorrect agent behavior.
title: [Q8] brain-check.sh: doesn't scan CLAUDE.md for broken links

close_reason: Analyzed: brain-refresh.sh runs in 2.7s manually on 72 files, codebase ceiling ~120 files. Complexity cost of optimization exceeds benefit. Premature optimization / over-engineering.
description:
Finding E2: Every Swift file has line count computed via wc -l in 3 separate generators (3N subshells for N files). Should run wc -l once across all files upfront and store in associative array.

DEFERRED: Sub-second at 72 files. Optimization matters at scale.
UPSIDE: Reduces 3N wc -l calls to 1 bulk call. Cleaner data flow.
CHURN RISK: Low — internal refactor, requires bash associative arrays (bash 4+, macOS ships zsh but bash 3.x — need to verify compatibility or use temp file approach).
REVISIT TRIGGER: Bundle with E1/E3 if doing a brain-refresh.sh optimization pass.
title: [E2] brain-refresh.sh: wc -l counted 3x per file

close_reason: Session summary captured. All deliverables committed and pushed.
description:
## Planning Council Session (2026-03-06)

### What Happened
- Spun up 7-agent planning council (Oracle, Architect, ChatterBox, Talent Team, Simplifier, Gap Finder, Talent Team 2)
- Produced comprehensive phased implementation plan for independent WhisperKit transcription highway
- External sign-off received from GPT-4.1 (whisperkit-architecture-debate buddies session)

### Deliverables
- **17 planning artifacts** committed to docs/plans/whisperkit-highway/ (3,591 lines)
- **5 new skills** created in .claude/skills/:
  1. wispr-scaffold-whisperkit-capture (→ audio-pipeline agent)
  2. wispr-scaffold-independent-pipeline (→ feature-scaffolding agent)
  3. wispr-configure-whisperkit-vad (→ audio-pipeline agent)
  4. wispr-configure-whisperkit-streaming (→ audio-pipeline agent)
  5. wispr-test-dual-pipeline (→ testing agent)
- **Brain files updated**: CLAUDE.md, task-router.md, feature-catalog.md, 3 agent definitions
- **CLAUDE.md audit**: 88/100 score

### Architecture Decisions
- DictationPipeline protocol + OverlayIntent enum (decoupled overlay from pipeline state)
- 8-state WhisperKitPipelineState: idle/loadingModel/ready/recording/transcribing/polishing/complete/error
- Event-driven handle(event: PipelineEvent) — prevents race conditions structurally
- Shared AudioCaptureManager (not separate instance)
- Single merge point: runTextProcessing(asrText:language:)
- Default model: small.en (large-v3 reframed as Offline/Max Quality)
- Languages: EN/DE/TA manual selection (not auto-detect)
- Backend switching: 7-step drain protocol with 5s timeout + zombie detection

### Key Discovery
- ew-9bd: LLMPolishStep ignores context.language — blocks WhisperKit multilingual. Scoped to Phase 4.

### 5-Phase Plan
- Phase 0: Foundation (DictationPipeline protocol + OverlayIntent)
- Phase 1: WhisperKitPipeline batch flow with .loadingModel state
- Phase 2: WhisperKit-native EnergyVAD + chunkingStrategy
- Phase 3: Streaming via AudioStreamTranscriber with batch fallback
- Phase 4: Polish convergence hardening + language contract fix

### 13 Oracle Guardrails (G1-G13)
All mapped to specific phases in master plan. Key: G1 (no overloaded states), G5 (OverlayIntent), G6 (model load doesn't block PTT), G9 (Parakeet untouched), G12 (tune before stream).

### Open Issues
- ew-9bd: LLMPolishStep language bug (P1, open)
- ew-u2d: Pipeline split epic (P1, open — ready to execute)
- ew-dvm: WhisperKit streaming (P2, blocked)

### Executive Presentation
- Building at docs/plans/whisperkit-highway/index.html (in progress)
design:

### Presentation (in progress)

close_reason: Analyzed: brain-refresh.sh runs in 2.7s manually on 72 files, codebase ceiling ~120 files. Complexity cost of optimization exceeds benefit. Premature optimization / over-engineering.
description:
Finding E8: 'xargs -0 -n1 dirname' spawns one dirname process per Swift file. Could use 'sed s|/[^/]*$||' as a single-process replacement.

DEFERRED: Trivial overhead at 72 files.
UPSIDE: N process spawns → 1 sed invocation. Clean improvement.
CHURN RISK: Very low — drop-in replacement, sed is portable.
REVISIT TRIGGER: Bundle with E1-E3 in optimization pass. Easiest of all efficiency fixes.
title: [E8] brain-refresh.sh: xargs dirname spawns N processes

close_reason: Not a real bug — Option PTT is intentional and short cold starts are expected. The 6-min recording was likely from wispr-eyes UAT test lingering.
description: Option-only PTT (keyCode=58) fires during normal typing/shortcuts. Caused a 6-min phantom recording (see logs 2026-03-07 00:02:58). Consider: minimum hold duration before PTT activates, double-tap requirement, or different default hotkey.
title: Accidental PTT: modifier-only hotkey too easy to trigger

description: Finding Q10: brain-check.sh code-fence stripping regex (sed '/^```/,/^```/d') assumes fences start at column 0. Indented fences or language-tagged fences not at column 0 could cause false-positive broken link reports. One false positive already hit and worked around. Fix is a 1-line regex improvement — do it next time brain-check.sh is touched for any reason. Not worth a standalone change.
title: [Q10] brain-check.sh: code-fence stripping regex is naive

description: Automatically detect whether user is in email (Mail, Gmail), messaging (Slack, iMessage), code editor (Cursor, VS Code), or general text field and adapt LLM polish formality/style accordingly. Wispr Flow does this with personalizationStyles (work/email/personal/other x formal/casual) stored per-transcript. SuperWhisper also has per-app profiles. Our implementation: use the app bundle ID we already capture in PasteService to route to different LLM prompt templates. Could start simple — map bundle IDs to categories, let user override. The auto-detection is the key differentiator vs manual switching. Ref: docs/competitors/wisprflow/deep-scan-2026-03.md §4 Personalization Styles, feature-requests/010-user-editable-llm-prompts.md.
title: Auto-context personalization — adapt polish style per app/environment

Save as WebP to docs/the-two-of-us-v2/public/scenes/scene-01-hats.webp through scene-10-horizon.webp. Style: dark purple background, warm lighting, manhwa/webtoon aesthetic, consistent character design. See plan for detailed prompts per scene.
title: Generate AI manhwa illustrations for 10 scenes

close_reason: LLMPolishStep now reads context.language via enrichedInstructions() — single enrichment point for all context-aware prompt modifications. Non-English transcripts get LANGUAGE preamble prepended to any prompt (default or custom). No protocol/connector changes. extensible for future guards (ew-zr4 short-transcript).
description: ChatterBox reality check found: LLMPolishStep never reads context.language. TranscriptPolisher.polish() has no language parameter. PolishInstructions has no language field. For English-only Parakeet this is invisible, but WhisperKit multilingual will send Japanese/French/German text to an English-assuming polish prompt, corrupting output. Fix: read context.language, prepend language context to prompt when non-en. File: LLMPolishStep.swift (~113 lines). This is the ONE adaptation needed at the merge point.
title: LLMPolishStep ignores language — blocks WhisperKit multilingual

close_reason: Implemented
description: See docs/feature-requests/008-custom-word-correction.md
title: Add custom word correction

close_reason: Subsumed into ew-byi settings refresh — environment presets implemented
description: Replace the raw VAD sensitivity slider with 3 environment presets: Quiet (library/quiet room), Normal (home/standard), Noisy (office/café). Each preset maps to tuned VAD parameters. Better UX — users pick their environment instead of tuning a technical slider. Competitors are doing this.
title: Replace VAD sensitivity slider with environment presets

description: WordCorrector currently splits input by whitespace and matches one token at a time. This means compound custom words like 'EnviousWispr' can never match when ASR outputs them as two tokens ('envious whisper'). Need a sliding window approach that considers adjacent token pairs/triples and can merge them into a single custom word replacement. Scoring engine (Levenshtein + bigram Dice + Soundex) is solid — just needs multi-token candidate generation.
title: Multi-token custom word matching

close_reason: Analyzed: brain-refresh.sh runs in 2.7s manually on 72 files, codebase ceiling ~120 files. Complexity cost of optimization exceeds benefit. Premature optimization / over-engineering.
description:
Finding E7: Four generators (file-index, type-index, task-router, feature-catalog) are independent but run sequentially. Could run in background subshells with 'wait'.

DEFERRED: Total runtime is sub-second. Parallelism adds complexity (background jobs, error handling, shared state concerns).
UPSIDE: ~4x wall-clock reduction (matters if codebase grows 10x+).
CHURN RISK: Medium — background subshells complicate error reporting. Must pre-compute shared data (E1-E4) before parallelizing, so this depends on those fixes.
REVISIT TRIGGER: Only after E1-E4 are done (they create the shared data model that enables clean parallelism).
title: [E7] brain-refresh.sh: no parallelism between generators

title: Polish: Scenes 01+02 strip SVG

description:
Finding Q5: brain-check.sh line 85 hardcodes 'file-index.md type-index.md task-router.md feature-catalog.md' — must stay in sync with brain-refresh.sh. If a 5th generated file is added, brain-check.sh won't know.

UPSIDE: Single source of truth for generated file names. Prevents sync bugs.

close_reason: Hardcoded optimal DecodingOptions (temp=0, fallback=3, skipSpecialTokens, suppressBlank, usePrefillPrompt/Cache). Removed Accuracy and Speech filter sliders from UI. Removed 4 WK settings properties from SettingsManager. Removed syncWhisperKitDecodingConfig from AppState.

Disable wordTimestamps by default. skipSpecialTokens=true. usePrefillPrompt=true. Remove user-facing Accuracy and Speech Filter sliders from UI — hardcode sensible defaults. Create golden test audio set (EN/DE/TA) to benchmark quality. Only large-v3 model — no cheaper options.

close_reason: Research complete. large-v3-turbo IS available in WhisperKit 0.15.0 (our resolved version) via string-based model names. No ModelVariant enum case, but WhisperKit.download(variant: "large-v3-turbo") and WhisperKitConfig(model: "large-v3-turbo") both work. Supported on all M-series Macs. Multilingual supported (it's based on large-v3 which is multilingual). Plan updated: default model changed from large-v3 to large-v3-turbo in Phase 1. Three hardcoded defaults to swap in SettingsManager, WhisperKitSetupService, WhisperKitBackend.
description:
SuperWhisper competitive analysis shows large-v3-turbo (their 'Ultra V3 Turbo') is the sweet spot: Speed 8/10, Accuracy 8/10. Our plan defaults to small.en (Speed 8, Accuracy 5 per SuperWhisper's ratings) — mediocre compared to competition.

Research needed:
1. Does WhisperKit 0.12+ support large-v3-turbo (openai/whisper-large-v3-turbo)?
2. What's the actual RTF on Apple Silicon (M1 Pro, M2, M3, M4)?
3. What's the model size / download size vs small.en?
4. Does it support multilingual (DE/TA) or English-only?
5. Is it available in WhisperKit's ModelVariant enum?
6. If available: update plan default from small.en to large-v3-turbo
7. If not available: file as dependency gap, check WhisperKit roadmap

Impact: If large-v3-turbo works in WhisperKit, it changes decision D7 in the master plan. We'd get near-large accuracy at dictation-usable speed — matching SuperWhisper's top offering.

Source: SuperWhisper model comparison screenshot (Desktop/Screenshot 2026-03-06 at 2.27.54 AM.png)
notes: User has personally tested SuperWhisper's Ultra V3 Turbo — confirms it's fast and supports multilingual. SuperWhisper uses whisper.cpp, not WhisperKit, so CoreML availability in WhisperKit must be independently verified. Expect same research depth as the Parakeet viability journey — multiple walls, wrong info, false assumptions before validation. Don't trust docs alone, must test on actual hardware.
title: Research: WhisperKit large-v3-turbo availability and benchmark

close_reason: Brain surgery complete: all 7 phases done, brain-check passes clean
description: Comprehensive brain audit completed 2026-03-06 with 6 Opus agents. Full findings in docs/brain-audit-report.md and docs/brain-audit-visualization.html (also in ~/Downloads/). Key findings: (1) Zero auto-update protocols across all 88 brain files. (2) 7 stale files with active data conflicts (SettingKey: 26/27/31 vs actual 34, file count 68 vs 72, roadmap says 8 implemented vs actual 16+). (3) Zero cascade mechanisms — Definition of Done missing brain maintenance steps. (4) Beads and knowledge files are isolated systems with no sync. (5) 48 plan files in docs/plans/ with zero brain refs. (6) 2 broken in-text skill refs (audit-actor-isolation). 12 prioritized recommendations in the report — 3 critical, 5 important, 4 enhancements. Start with critical items: fix data conflicts, add brain maintenance to DoD, fix agent factual errors.
notes: SESSION 2 UPDATE (2026-03-06): Council consultation started. Gemini 2.5 Pro provided comprehensive feedback — key insight: reorder to fix agent errors FIRST (active bugs), then build SSoT philosophy + brain-refresh automation BEFORE patching stale data. Recommends: (1) brain-integrity-check skill as ongoing test suite, (2) regenerate derivative files not patch them, (3) start brain-refresh manual then graduate to pre-commit hook, (4) golden path test prompts before/after surgery. OpenAI gpt-5.4-pro Responses API wired but MCP server needs restart to pick up new provider code — council consultation incomplete. NEXT: restart MCP, get OpenAI council input, synthesize both, enter plan mode, populate beads.
title: Brain surgery: fix drift, add cascades, resolve conflicts

close_reason: Fixed in 2fd9fc5. Added abortPreWarm() to AudioCaptureManager, cancellation cleanup in AppState.onStartRecording, Task.isCancelled guard in WhisperKitPipeline.preWarmAudioInput, defense-in-depth in both pipelines' requestStop(). UAT passed: quick tap, 8x spam, 2s hold — all clean.
description: Quick Option key tap in PTT mode starts audio engine via preWarm() but cancellation + requestStop() never cleans it up because pipeline state is still .idle. Engine stays running (macOS yellow mic indicator visible) with no recording or overlay. Fixed by adding AudioCaptureManager.abortPreWarm(), cancellation cleanup in onStartRecording, Task.isCancelled guard in WhisperKitPipeline.preWarmAudioInput, and defense-in-depth in both pipelines' requestStop().
title: PTT quick tap leaks audio engine (yellow mic indicator)

close_reason: Implemented: 5-min graceful auto-stop in both pipeline VAD loops via TimingConstants.maxRecordingDuration. Calls stopAndTranscribe() instead of emergency teardown.
description: A forgotten or accidental recording runs forever, consuming memory and CPU (95 incremental decodes over 6 min). Add a configurable max duration (e.g. 5 min default) that auto-stops recording with a notification.
title: No max recording duration safety limit

close_reason: Fixed PTT race: (1) clear stopRequested at startRecording() entry to wipe stale flags from previous cycles, (2) tighten requestStop() to only set flag during .idle/.loadingModel — ignore during .polishing/.transcribing/.complete/.error. Root cause: key-up during polish set stopRequested, which persisted and aborted every subsequent recording.
description: When user presses PTT again while pipeline is still processing a previous recording (polish/paste phase), the pipeline enters a bad state where all subsequent recordings are 0.00s and immediately discarded. Requires app restart to recover. Root cause: pipeline doesn't fully reset before accepting new recording when mid-polish. Seen in logs: PTT press at 18:59:44 while previous recording's polish was in-flight, then all subsequent attempts are 0.00s. Phase 1 bug cleanup item.
notes: App development bug — phase 1 cleanup alongside ew-pe9, ew-9bd. NOT part of brain surgery epic (ew-cbo).
title: Pipeline race: rapid PTT re-press during polish causes permanent 0.00s recording

Ref: docs/competitors/wisprflow/deep-scan-2026-03.md §4 Dictionary.
title: Dictionary with custom words and snippet expansion

close_reason: Pre-load implemented and verified. Model loads silently after download via withObservationTracking on setupState. Also triggers on app launch if model already cached. detectState() call added to init to bootstrap observation. UAT: wispr-eyes confirms 'WhisperKit · Loaded' on launch.
description: WhisperKitSetupService downloads model to disk but never calls backend.prepare(). First recording pays full WhisperKit(config) cost (~30-60s for large-v3-turbo). Fix: after download completes, have AppState trigger backend.prepare() via the pipeline — mirrors Parakeet's onboarding pre-load pattern. Keep existing .never unload policy. Surgical change in AppState only.
title: WhisperKit: pre-load model after download to eliminate cold-start delay

close_reason: Fixed: added transitionToRecording() method to RecordingOverlayPanel. The bug was that show(audioLevelProvider:) returned early when a panel already existed (the Starting... polishing panel), with no transition path to recording. Now mirrors transitionToPolishing() in reverse.
description: After fixing .startingUp to show 'Starting...' overlay, the overlay never transitions to the recording state. Recording works correctly underneath but the overlay remains on Starting... for the full duration. Likely the overlay update isn't being triggered when state transitions from .startingUp to .recording — the onStateChange callback may not fire for this transition, or the RecordingOverlayPanel isn't re-evaluating overlayIntent.
title: Bug: overlay stuck on 'Starting...' for entire WhisperKit recording

close_reason: Already implemented — incremental worker, tail decode, clipTimestamps, language-aware polish all in codebase
description: Background worker periodically transcribes growing audio buffer during recording. Adaptive: full-buffer retranscription for <30s, clipTimestamps for >30s. When user stops, recent result often already available — skip batch ASR, go straight to polish. Safe batch fallback if worker result stale/empty. No UI changes, no streaming model.
title: WhisperKit Highway Phase 3: Background Incremental Transcription

close_reason: Fixed: preWarmAudioInput() now fire-and-forget calls backend.prepare()
description: preWarmAudioInput() only warms audio capture, not the WhisperKit model. Add backend.prepare() call as defense-in-depth — catches cases where model was unloaded by policy timer or memory pressure. Idempotent (no-op if already ready). Deferred to Phase 2 performance work.
title: WhisperKit: pre-warm model on PTT key-down as safety net

close_reason: All 19 stale TRACKER.md refs updated across 9 files. P0 (3 files, 12 refs), P1 (4 files, 4 refs), plus governance doc fix and feature-tracker DoD update.
description: 19 stale TRACKER.md refs across 9 files need updating to use Beads. See docs/plans/beads-cleanup-plan.md for full details.
title: Fix stale TRACKER.md references after Beads adoption

close_reason: Eliminated stopRequested flag from WhisperKitPipeline. Added .startingUp state to WhisperKitPipelineState enum + isActive + overlayIntent. requestStop() during .startingUp/.loadingModel sets state=.idle. startRecording() checks state after each await suspension point. AppState refactored with activePipeline computed property routing through DictationPipeline protocol.
description: The stopRequested boolean is a side-channel that operates outside the pipeline state machine. It was the root cause of ew-d17 (PTT race → permanent 0.00s recordings). Current fix (clear on entry + tighten requestStop) is correct but the pattern is fragile — every new await in startRecording() needs a manual checkpoint, and the flag is invisible to the state machine. Refactor: add .startingUp state to WhisperKitPipelineState and PipelineState. requestStop() during .startingUp transitions directly to .idle — no boolean flag needed. Eliminates the entire class of stale-flag bugs. Should be done before Phase 2/3 (VAD, streaming) adds more async checkpoints to startRecording().
title: Refactor: eliminate stopRequested flag — add .startingUp state to pipeline state machines

close_reason: Caused by accidental 6-min recording from UAT test. Normal recordings are short — extended thinking + MAX_TOKENS is not an issue at typical transcript lengths. Revisit if users report slow polish on normal recordings.
description: 1117 chars ASR input produced 30042 chars Gemini output before being truncated at MAX_TOKENS (8192 tokens). gemini-2.5-flash with extended thinking may be burning tokens on reasoning. Investigate: prompt engineering, output token cap, or model selection for short vs long inputs.
title: Gemini LLM polish blowup: 27x output expansion hitting MAX_TOKENS

close_reason: Intent-driven overlay implemented. RecordingOverlayPanel.show(intent:, audioLevelProvider:) unified API with flicker guard. PolishingOverlayView accepts dynamic label. AppState reads overlayIntent from both pipelines — loadingModel shows 'Loading model...', transcribing shows 'Transcribing...', polishing shows 'Polishing...'. Build passes, app running.
description: AppState.onStateChange calls showPolishing() for .loadingModel, .transcribing, and .polishing — all show the same 'Polishing' spinner regardless of actual state. The plan (G5) says overlay should observe OverlayIntent directly. Fix: add show(intent: OverlayIntent) to RecordingOverlayPanel that renders the correct label from .processing(label:). Eliminates entire class of wrong-label bugs. Both pipelines use same overlay API via overlayIntent property. Needs council review for architectural approach.
notes: Council review (o3 + gemini) COMPLETE. Decisions: (1) Keep OverlayIntent Equatable — pass audioLevelProvider as separate param to show(intent:, audioLevelProvider:). (2) Retrofit showPolishing() to showPolishing(label: String = 'Polishing...') — one code path for generation tokens. (3) PolishingOverlayView already has label text — make it dynamic, no new view. (4) Guard against identical intents to prevent flicker. (5) Migrate both pipelines in one commit, delete legacy API after.
title: Overlay: replace hardcoded showPolishing() with intent-driven show(intent:) system

close_reason: Replaced hardcoded 150ms sleep with polling loop that verifies target app is frontmost before pasting (50ms polls, 500ms timeout). Logs a warning if activation fails.
description: The 150ms hardcoded sleep after app.activate() is insufficient when the user tabs away during longer recordings. The target app may not be fully foregrounded before Cmd+V fires, causing the paste to silently fail (transcript saves to history but doesn't paste). Fix: verify frontmost app matches targetApp before pasting, with a retry/backoff loop instead of a fixed sleep.
title: Fix paste reliability when user switches apps during recording

description: brain-refresh.sh generates a Source File Map in task-router.md (lines 9-131, ~122 lines) that duplicates file-index.md. Task-router already links to file-index.md. Remove the auto-generated source file map from task-router.md generation to save ~120 lines of context.
title: Remove source file map duplication from task-router.md generation

close_reason: Analyzed: brain-refresh.sh runs in 2.7s manually on 72 files, codebase ceiling ~120 files. Complexity cost of optimization exceeds benefit. Premature optimization / over-engineering.
description:
Finding E4: generate_type_index (line 144) runs grep -rnE for type declarations. generate_feature_catalog_generated (line 245) runs nearly identical grep -rcE for count. Two full source tree scans with same pattern.

DEFERRED: Two greps vs one is negligible at 72 files.
UPSIDE: Halves type-declaration scanning. Could share results between generators.
CHURN RISK: Medium — requires restructuring data flow between generators, or pre-computing type data as shared state. More invasive than E1-E3.
REVISIT TRIGGER: If type-index generation becomes slow, or if adding more type-derived outputs.
title: [E4] brain-refresh.sh: type-declaration grep runs twice

description: When dictating after existing text (e.g., after a period at the end of a sentence), the pasted transcript touches the preceding character with no space. Expected: automatic leading space so 'sentence.Hello' becomes 'sentence. Hello'. Wispr Flow handles this — likely by checking the character before the cursor via AX and prepending a space if it's not whitespace/newline.
title: Paste doesn't prepend space after existing text

close_reason: Planning council complete — all research incorporated into master plan
description: Define exact data contract at the merge point. Required transcript format, metadata expectations, timing/async considerations, error propagation. This is the ONLY allowed convergence point.
title: Research: Polish LLM merge contract (data format, metadata, async, errors)

close_reason: Analyzed: brain-refresh.sh runs in 2.7s manually on 72 files, codebase ceiling ~120 files. Complexity cost of optimization exceeds benefit. Premature optimization / over-engineering.
description:
Finding E5: Type-index loop spawns 6 subshells per grep match (3x cut, 2x grep, 1x awk). For 163 type declarations, that's ~978 subshells. Could be replaced with single awk pipeline.

DEFERRED: ~1s overhead at current scale. Awk replacement is more complex to maintain.
UPSIDE: Eliminates all per-line subshells. Significant speedup at scale.
CHURN RISK: Medium — awk pipeline is harder to read/debug than sequential cut/grep. Trade readability for performance.
REVISIT TRIGGER: If type count exceeds ~500, or if bundled with E1-E4 optimization pass.
title: [E5] brain-refresh.sh: 6 subshells per type-declaration line

close_reason: Root cause: WhisperKit windowClipTime=1.0s skips last 1s of audio. Fix: (1) set windowClipTime=0 in makeDecodeOptions and tail decode, (2) append 500ms silence padding in WhisperKitBackend.padAudioWithSilence() applied to both batch transcribe and incremental tail decode. Silence padding gives decoder look-ahead context; windowClipTime=0 ensures no real audio is skipped.
description: Root cause: WhisperKit windowClipTime=1.0s skips last 1s of audio. Fix: (1) append 500ms silence padding before final decode, (2) set windowClipTime=0 on final decode options. Applies to both tail decode in worker and batch fallback in backend.
title: Fix WhisperKit end-of-speech truncation (silence padding + windowClipTime)

close_reason: Analyzed: brain-refresh.sh runs in 2.7s manually on 72 files, codebase ceiling ~120 files. Complexity cost of optimization exceeds benefit. Premature optimization / over-engineering.
description:
Finding E3: Inside each directory loop, 'find dir -maxdepth 1 -name *.swift' runs per-directory in all 3 generators. The initial find already located every file — grouping by dirname in a single pass would eliminate these.

DEFERRED: Marginal gain at current scale.
UPSIDE: Eliminates N extra find invocations per generator.
CHURN RISK: Low — pairs naturally with E1/E2.
REVISIT TRIGGER: Bundle with E1/E2 in a single optimization pass.
title: [E3] brain-refresh.sh: redundant per-directory find inside loops

close_reason: Two-layer fix: (1) hard cutoff ≤3 words skips LLM entirely — pass through verbatim, (2) prompt reinforcement for 4-10 words tells LLM to return short inputs as-is. Both in LLMPolishStep. Thresholds tunable via minWordsForPolish constant.
description: When ASR captures only a filler word like 'Yeah' (accidental short recording), the LLM polish step generates a full conversational response instead of passing through or discarding. Screenshot shows 'Yeah.' becoming a multi-paragraph speech about public speaking. Root cause: polish prompt gives the LLM too much creative latitude on minimal input. Fix options: (1) short-circuit polish for transcripts under N words/chars, (2) add explicit instruction in polish prompt to preserve ultra-short inputs verbatim, (3) both. This was not an issue before — may be related to a model change (appears to be Gemini from the style). Post-highway cleanup item.
title: LLM polish hallucinates on ultra-short transcripts (e.g. 'Yeah')

close_reason: Tiered paste system implemented and verified: AX direct → CGEvent → AppleScript → clipboard fallback
description:
Implement tiered paste system to replace single-method CGEvent Cmd+V approach. Validated by Gemini + OpenAI.

ARCHITECTURE (ordered cascade):
- Tier 1: AX direct insertion via kAXSelectedTextAttribute on captured AXFocusedUIElement (zero disruption, no clipboard, no focus change)
- Tier 2: Activate app + CGEvent Cmd+V via .cgAnnotatedSessionEventTap (current approach, refined with AXObserver-based paste confirmation)
- Tier 2b: AppleScript Edit > Paste (sandboxed app fallback, requires frontmost)  
- Tier 3: Clipboard + user notification (graceful failure, text never lost)

KEY IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Capture AXFocusedUIElement at PTT start (alongside existing NSRunningApplication)
- All AX calls on MainActor (AXUIElement is Sendable but operations are not thread-safe)
- Set AXTimeout(1.0s) before AX writes to avoid 30s hang on misbehaving apps
- Verify kAXRoleAttribute == textField/textArea before Tier 1 attempt
- Log (bundle_id, successful_tier, duration_ms) for compatibility analytics
- Switch CGEvent tap from .cghidEventTap to .cgAnnotatedSessionEventTap

FILES TO MODIFY:
1. TranscriptionPipeline.swift — add targetElement capture + tiered paste cascade
2. PasteService.swift — add insertViaAccessibility(), pasteViaAppleScript(), switch event tap, add AXObserver paste confirmation
3. Constants.swift — paste confirmation polling constants

SOURCES: Hex (kitlangton/Hex), speak2 (zachswift615/speak2), Gemini, OpenAI
title: Implement tiered paste system (AX direct → CGEvent → AppleScript → clipboard)
